report_name: FDD Warns Weakening CISA Threatens US National Security

threat_actors.name: Volt Typhoon
threat_actors.description: A Chinese state-sponsored group that was found to be pre-positioning itself within U.S. critical infrastructure networks, cited as an example of the threats CISA is positioned to counter.
threat_actors.goals: Pre-positioning within critical infrastructure for potential future disruption.

identities.name: Foundation for Defense of Democracies (FDD)
identities.description: A national security think tank that issued a warning against weakening the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA).

identities.name: U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA)
identities.description: The U.S. federal agency responsible for cybersecurity and infrastructure protection, which is the subject of a warning about the potential negative impacts on national security if its authority is weakened.

identities.name: Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) Agencies
identities.description: A class of U.S. government agencies that CISA protects and issues Binding Operational Directives (BODs) to, such as patching vulnerabilities from the KEV catalog.

identities.name: State, Local, Tribal, and Territorial (SLTT) Governments
identities.description: A class of U.S. government entities that rely on CISA's guidance and protection.

identities.name: U.S. Critical Infrastructure Sectors
identities.description: The 16 critical infrastructure sectors in the U.S. that CISA helps protect and which have been targeted by groups like Volt Typhoon.

relationships.source_name: U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA)
